comparison: Tool Calling Flow Comparison: VSCode Native vs OpenClaude vs Universal LLM Provider

vscode_native.description: How VSCode's LanguageModelChatProvider API works with supported models (OpenRouter, GitHub Copilot)
steps: User sends message → VSCode Chat UI
VSCode collects tool definitions from MCP servers
VSCode calls provider.provideLanguageModelChatResponse(model, messages, options)
options.tools: array from connected MCP servers (passed EVERY invocation)
Provider sends POST /v1/chat/completions with: messages[], tools[], stream:true
Provider receives SSE stream
delta.content → LanguageModelTextPart
delta.tool_calls → LanguageModelToolCallPart
VSCode executes tool (LanguageModelToolCallPart → MCP execution)
Tool result → LanguageModelToolResultPart back to provider
VSCode calls provideLanguageModelChatResponse() AGAIN with tool results
options.tools still present on every invocation
Repeat until model responds with text only (no tool_calls)

openclaude.description: How OpenClaude's openaiShim.ts (1098 lines) handles tool calling
steps: Claude Code calls Anthropic SDK client.messages.create()
openaiShim intercepts this call
Converts Anthropic system prompts → OpenAI role:system message
Converts Anthropic content blocks block-by-block:
- text blocks → assistant content string or array
- tool_use blocks → assistant.tool_calls[] array items
- tool_result blocks → separate role:tool messages with tool_call_id
- thinking blocks → <thinking> tags in text content
- image blocks → image_url parts in content array
Converts Anthropic tools (name, description, input_schema) → OpenAI tools array
- Normalizes schemas: adds required[], additionalProperties: false
POST to any OpenAI-compatible API with messages[], tools[]
Processes SSE stream with activeToolCalls Map for accumulation
- tool_calls come across multiple chunks (id+name then arguments)
- Accumulates partial arguments in jsonBuffer per tool index
- Auto-completes malformed JSON at finish (tries closing brackets)
Converts OpenAI streaming events back to Anthropic format
Claude Code processes tool_use events, executes tools, returns results
Tool results appended as tool_result blocks → next SDK call

key_patterns.accumulation: activeToolCalls Map<index, {id, name, jsonBuffer}> — accumulates across chunks
key_patterns.content_handling: Separates tool_use from text blocks, handles images, thinking

our_extension.description: Current state of Universal LLM Provider extension (396 lines)
steps: VSCode calls provideLanguageModelChatResponse()
mapMessage() converts VSCode message parts to OpenAI format
For native models: injects tools[] array in request body
POST to provider API with messages[], (tools[] if native), stream:true
Stream loop processes chunks:
- delta.content → LanguageModelTextPart
- delta.tool_calls → LanguageModelToolCallPart (IF name AND arguments in same chunk)
After stream: fallback parser for non-native models only
Fallback: XML/JSON parser extracts tool calls from accumulated text

bugs.title: No streaming tool call accumulation
bugs.problem: DeepSeek sends tool calls across multiple SSE chunks. First chunk has id+name with empty arguments (''). Subsequent chunks have arguments without name. Our check requires BOTH in same chunk, so the tool call is NEVER emitted.
chunk_sequence: Chunk 1: { tool_calls: [{ index:0, id:'call_1', function:{ name:'list_dir', arguments:'' } }] }  ← SKIPPED (arguments is falsy)
Chunk 2: { tool_calls: [{ index:0, function:{ arguments:'{"path":' } }] }  ← SKIPPED (name undefined)
Chunk 3: { tool_calls: [{ index:0, function:{ arguments:'"/tmp"}' } }] }  ← SKIPPED (name undefined)
Result: Tool call never emitted to VSCode
bugs.fix: Implement activeToolCalls Map<index, {id, name, jsonBuffer}> keyed by tc.index, like OpenClaude. Initialize on id+name chunk, append on arguments chunks, emit on finish_reason:'tool_calls' or when JSON is parseable.
bugs.severity: CRITICAL — Makes tool calling non-functional for DeepSeek

bugs.title: Early return in mapMessage for tool results
bugs.problem: Returns on FIRST ToolResultPart. Parallel tool calls produce multiple results in one message; only the first is mapped.
bugs.fix: Collect all tool results in an array, return all of them (or flatten into multiple messages).
bugs.severity: HIGH when using parallel tool calls

bugs.title: No schema normalization for tool parameters
bugs.problem: OpenAI/DeepSeek strict mode requires additionalProperties:false and all properties in required[]. MCP schemas may not comply.
bugs.fix: Normalize schemas: ensure all property keys are in required[], set additionalProperties:false on all object types.
bugs.severity: LOW — may cause 400 errors with strict mode enabled

bugs.title: content: null already fixed
bugs.current_code: return { role, content: null, tool_calls: toolCalls };
bugs.status: FIXED — Was content: '', now content: null matching OpenAI standard
bugs.severity: WAS CRITICAL — DeepSeek requires null for assistant tool call messages

tools_on_every_call.vscode_native: Yes — VSCode passes on every invocation
tools_on_every_call.openclaude: Yes — Claude Code sends every request
tools_on_every_call.our_extension: Yes — re-reads from options.tools each call

streaming_accumulation.our_extension: None — requires name+args in same chunk ❌

parallel_tool_calls.our_extension: Early return — only first tool result ❌

content_for_tool_messages.vscode_native: null for assistant, string for tool
content_for_tool_messages.openclaude: null for assistant, string for tool

recommendations.action: Implement streaming tool call accumulation (activeToolCalls Map)
recommendations.reason: Without this, DeepSeek tool calls are never emitted to VSCode

recommendations.action: Fix early return in mapMessage for multiple tool results
recommendations.reason: Required for parallel tool call execution

recommendations.action: Add schema normalization for tool parameters
recommendations.reason: Prevents 400 errors with strict mode providers

recommendations.action: Consider removing fallback parser entirely for native models
recommendations.reason: Unused code path for models that support native tool calling
